title: DO NOT SUBSCRIBE.
review:
This app charged me for a subscription I never signed up for. I had to cancel my credit card because the charges kept coming. Even after I canceled all subscriptions, they charged me again using my new credit card. Now I have to apply for another credit card because of this issue.

This has been extremely frustrating and stressful. Be very careful before subscribing because it is very difficult to stop the charges.

title: Interesting
review:
Really like the app, even though I sometimes disagree with its methodology. I’m currently reading Never Split the Difference by Chris Voss, and I prefer his approach over this one. His techniques feel more practical and less formal. 

Even so, this app is still very helpful — just not as strong as the methods taught by Chris Voss, a former FBI negotiator.

If you’re looking for a solid program to improve your speech, I would still highly recommend it.

title: Congratulations! You successfully unlocked a new level of predatory subscription!
review: If you can’t pull off designing a clear subscription path you can’t be trusted with the rest of your product. You are either amazing scammers who are intentionally trapping people and hope they never figure it out. Or you have no idea what you are doing.

title: Horrible, la IA no te entiende, la app se traba y los ejercicios son siempre iguales

Da ejercicios inutiles y te hace hacer los mismos ejercicios siempre, con los textos identicos y a veces mas de una vez porque no toma el resultado. Si le pones que un ejercicio no te sirve, se traba y te lo hace hacer de nuevo. Es dinero tirado a la basura.  Ni intenten.

title: The app  was much better several years ago

It looks strange, it feels strange. The facial expression looks awkward (especially in the exercise lullaby). A lot of bugs in the section “Peter communication couch”. Freeze, doesn't work correctly.

title: WARNING subscription scam

When I disputed the unexpected charge, they did cancel the subscription and refund the money, hence 2 stars instead of 1.

title: Free tier changes
review: If I hadn't experienced this app before, I wouldn't have the premium version. The current free version is sooo limited to what it used to offer. It seems like every action prompts the user to upgrade and begins the subscription flow. I don't think it's accurate to describe this as freemium if one were to do so. This version of the app also heavily pushes the user to focus on social skills rather than the ones they actually want to develop. The customer service is great though. So if you're wondering if the premium is worth it, I think so. But you wouldn't know it based on the free version.

title: Stupid

review: Doesn’t match my collaborative - assertive style and pushes a strongly assertive / adversarial register. I’d value more scenarios and leeway calibrated around consensus, building, motivation understanding.

title: Fraudulent charges
review: I must have downloaded this app at some point. They are charging me weekly. There is no record of the subscription in my Apple account. I never created a login and when I try to login with the only email associated with Apple account, there is no record of a subscription to cancel. There is no support email, only a chat bot that says they will respond in 48 hours. I’ve been sending emails for over a week with no response. Can someone at this company please reach out and do your duty to support. This is a dark pattern and apps like this should be banned from the App Store.

title: It’s changed
review: This really helped my voice range and quality for speaking and singing. Was away for a year and it’s entirely changed. Exercises in making conversation with an AI (who doesn’t understand conversation at all) and videos about succeeding at the office. It’s not a product that is useful to me anymore and there are way too many things out there already purporting to increase career and personal success. Wish they’d not undermined the integrity of this app by changing it into something different. Will be deleting.

title: No teaching
review: The app is not giving me specific feedback or pointers.  It just says good job or try again.  It’s not very help.

title: scam
review: do not pay for this. i have canceled several times and continue to get charged.

title: Will never know what this app contains
review: The onboarding brainwashing is unreasonably long. Plus there's no way to preview the content without paying at least $10. I'll have to assume this app is for ESL's, transsexuals, children, and people with speech impediments rather than everyday people who just want to make small tweaks to their speaking voice.

title: Great if they fixed the bugs, just not in 2026
review:
Useful exercises. You can have your voice rated anytime on three scores. Clarity, tempo and variety. Except you can’t. In an April upgrade, they introduced a feature where you automatically get a 99 or 100% clarity score. User support has brushed aside reports of the bug claiming that it affects SOME of the users SOME of the time. Well it’s more like ALL of the time.

The other knock is that the supposedly AI coach is not much AI or a coach. It just gives canned advice, not tailored to each of us.

The concept behind the app is gold. The execution more like deadwood.

title: Absolute scam — paid for nothing, support nonexistent
review:
I’m furious. I paid for a weekly subscription, and the app is completely unusable. Lessons won’t load, tests get stuck “processing” for ages, and then just throw a “Processing error” message. Nothing works.

To make it worse, I can’t even reach support — either the page is a white screen of death, or the input window just hangs. And when I finally managed to type out my issue, I got “Failed to submit ticket.” ARE YOU KIDDING ME?

This happens on every network — different WiFi connections, mobile data, you name it. My internet is fast and stable, so don’t even try to blame my connection.

You’re happily taking people’s money for a broken product with zero way to ask for help. That’s not a bug — that’s a scam. Avoid this app at all costs.

title: Worst app ever
review: Worst app ever! Totally scammed me into thinking I was getting a week free and when charged me for a month and blocked my account on Apple. Such a scam! Don’t trust this app!!!!

title: This new Vocal Image role-play is really helpful
review: It would be better if we have articulation correction as well. More like accent reduction and how to project my voice while speaking to make it more sound like American. That is one of the key area. I have been struggling and did not course online or through apps

title: Deceptive
review: I cancelled my subscription well before the end of the 7 free day trial.  The subscription no longer shows in my Apple subscription list. However, I keep getting charged weekly on my CC.

title: Yearly subscription I didn’t want
review: I don’t know if this app is actually good for anything. It’s a good idea, but I don’t think it actually helps you at all. Also, I feel like I got scammed into a yearly subscription that I didn’t want. I just wanted to try it out but very quickly. I accidentally had purchased the yearly subscription. Lame.

title: -25 Star review, this is the worst
review:
I originally signed up for Vocal Image Review at $29.99 just to try it out. What I didn’t realize is that they would start charging $59.99 every 3 months—timed just far enough apart that it’s easy to miss on your bank statements.

This feels like predatory subscription behavior. There was no clear, upfront communication about this recurring billing, and it’s clearly designed to slip under the radar.

What’s even more concerning is that I noticed the charge was initially declined through Apple and then immediately reprocessed and approved right after—without any action on my end. That raises serious concerns about how aggressively these charges are being pushed through.

On top of that, the software itself is not nearly valuable enough to justify the cost—not even close to $59, let alone on a recurring basis.

Be very careful before signing up. Check your statements and make sure you cancel if you’re not actively using it.

title: Random and aimless

If you want help articulating your questions or ideas  and reducing your filler words for professional reasons, this app doesn’t seem to be it

I am truly grateful for this app for showing me my areas I needed to work on and also give me the confidence on areas I was doubting myself and didn’t realize I was already good at. Not only does this app help you improve your speech but your social skills and thinking outside the box for those times you might come across a difficult situation or conversation.

title: Good concept but strange execution.
review: This application requested my review after completing the initial two exercises and I am ready to delete it. The first two exercises proved to be the most perplexing and nonsensical tasks I have encountered. They were unusually strange, my first task, I had to seek assistance in a highly vague scenario. I managed to proceed only by becoming increasingly irate and frustrated with the AI situation, ultimately scoring eight out of ten. I would not have handled this situation in the same manner IRL. The application required me to perform speech training, which involved three minutes of what can only be described as dangerous hyperventilation exercises while standing. I question the safety of this practice. Furthermore, it provided some metrics without any prior comparison, merely offering an objective analysis of my voice's sound without reference to a baseline.

You don’t get to read an excerpt in social situations in the real world.

This is the first day of using this app. And it has already frustrating to no end. Here are my observations : 

1. The upsells that try to sell you what they promise you the app already does.  Ridiculous.  
To get what they tell you the app does, you need to pay 3x what the app costs???? 

2. The absurdly unnecessary lessons with a looooong load time PLUS the intro music and graphics.  The extraordinarily slow pace of the very very basic lessons, that only require a sentence. 

3. Going into the lessons. It asks you to speak a sentence. And processes and analyzes forever.  Comes back with something went wrong. Do it again. 

4. The accent. I think if yo uare promising accent correction, the least you could do is having a good American or British accent.  

5. The AI modeling so far is in no way a public speaking model.  

This app sounds nothing like the ads that advertised it.

title: LOL @ the track suit lady
review:
The immediate and detailed feedback is incredibly helpful. I definitely believe my voice has improved as a result of this app. The vocal exercises are distracting because they’re explained only in writing by little cartoon people and then all of a sudden the same lady in blue wearing the same hood-up wind-tunnel half-zipped track-suit onesie appears. The exercises are silly enough as it is (think: monosyllabic tribal chants) so it would help if there were a variety of people taking them seriously. Alas there is there only ONE human depicted and the one they picked for that singular VOCAL COACH role so happens to have an incredibly thick accent? But it’s not the accent that is most distracting… Tell me: Does she have only one blue onesie? Did she film it all at once or did she take breaks? Does the blue onesie get laundered?
Or is it a uniform as in there are several blue onesies? Does she have other clothing? Does she get to take breaks for meals? I must know more about her… in all seriousness, I admire the effort she puts into her trade but it’s just not resonating with me anytime after time, and I skip ahead. Other than that, the program is fantastic.

title: Highly recommend
review:
I’ve been using Vocal Image for just over three weeks now, and I can honestly say it has become one of the highlights of my day. From the very beginning, I looked forward to practicing with the app, and for the first 15 days it became a daily ritual that was both enjoyable and motivating. Now on day 22, I’m even more excited to continue, as I can already feel the gradual transformation in my voice.

What I Love About Vocal Image

Well-Organized & Easy to Use: The app is structured in a way that makes progress feel natural and achievable.
Bite-Sized Learning: Each session is short, focused, and packed with valuable insights, making it easy to stay consistent.
Motivational & Encouraging: The positive words and tips truly make a difference, helping me stay inspired to show up every day.
Visible Progress: By practicing daily, I can already sense improvements in my voice, which keeps me motivated to continue.

Vocal Image is more than just an app—it feels like a supportive coach guiding me step by step. I’m grateful for the encouragement it provides and the confidence it’s helping me build. I look forward to seeing how much more my voice will grow as I stay committed to this journey.

⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️ — Highly recommended for anyone who wants to transform their voice with consistency and positivity!

title: A bit of a scam, but not useless
review:
This app is a bit of a scam. Not worthless, but certainly not worth the subscription. The app guides you through vocal exercises, recording you reading a passage before and after each exercise, then shows you some metrics about pitch, pacing, and range, that you can use to gauge your own progress. The exercises are generally helpful, although as far as I can tell, they are just cycled  randomly regardless of your goals, prior results, or progress.

The “AI coach” feature is useless. It hallucinates filler words, and doesn’t really give advice tailored to your submitted recordings. It feels like random vocal advice.

Each exercise comes with 3 steps of written instructions, followed by a video demonstration of the exercise that you are meant to mirror. The instructions often don’t describe what the demonstrator is actually doing. I suspect the developers of the app input the demo videos to an LLM, asked it to write a three step set of instructions for each, then added them to the app without doing an QA due diligence. You can still mirror the video. But the fact that you have to ignore the textual instructions is insulting.

The value of this app is that it prompts you to do a daily series of vocal exercises, and provides you with metrics. They will probably improve your speaking voice.  But my dissatisfaction lead me to search online for other exercises that would focus on specific areas I wanted to improve. It turns out there are a lot them for free. Many are more effective.

title: Pretty good, AI needs work

It’s something that you need to do consistently but when I do the daily exercises, I do notice a improvement in my voice, and those around me have commented on my voice as well. However, the AI has some drawbacks. For example, it identifies the words you read out from the passage as filler words. It’s worth taking the AI coach with a pinch of salt but overall for the price I think it’s very helpful. I prefer this over reading a book about vocal training because it’s very bite-size and easy to digest and make a habit out of it.

title: Could have used a little more attention. Ascetic, from afar.
review:
Has some good concepts but I believe they were executed lazily.

The app I believe is definitely proportionate to an app which would be funded through ad revenue, not subscription and feature blocks. The app is lain out pretty well; it looks great but it appears to be more aesthetic than authentic. If it were given a better AI, with more diversity in it such as: Two sex instructors, some pictures with the vague instructions, a definition via “question mark” that shows you what a larynx is. I didn’t, it would be convenient, if it wasn’t so vague in description and easily deduced through picture or information it would be negligible.

The design of the app 

1. The design in the lessons has you record your voice before the exercise after it shows instructions, written instruction that is hardly explanatory in how the exercise should be done. The woman who is supposed to be the trainer simply starts instantly without showing the movements in accordance to the written instructions. “Pull your shoulders in” that is pretty vague, it then shows a woman doing the “f” exercise at an angle where you can’t even tell if her shoulders are “pulled” in. 

2. The trainer needs to be a man for men. The “M” exercise that says “ feel the m vibrate in the lips” is vague and you can’t distinguish exactly how the “m” is supposed to be when you’re using the opposite sex as an example. 

The AI ability to record and display the voice Data:

The 

1. The test you accent part I will say is pretty nonsense which makes me very doubtful over any of the apps credibility in judging the voice in every aspect. I am very American sounding, it says I’m English! I proceed to portray my MOST COCKNEY British accent I can; behold it thinks I’m American!

2. I am pretty doubtful over it’s ability in distinguishing the difference between masculinity and femininity; I see it being pretty backwards here as well. Along with what the AI’s impression over what “confidence” sounds like. 

In conclusion with these types of issue especially the blatantly poor ability in the AI’s data; I can envision the further lessons being disappointing. I imagine that without some assurance in the ai, id be pretty dissatisfied with too much effort or investment. Good concept, needs revision.

I was a little skeptical when I began this, but am noticing improvements quickly.

The coaching tips are great.  Things I’ve never known that impact immediately.

title: Finding my very own voice!
review: I had use this to find my very own voice to describe my life experience. I would say this would help you guys whoever has depression this will help find you for who you are in this world that we call reality. So if your feeling down or have depression than this app will guide you to the right way to find your voice. Not a doctor, I once had it. I found my voice thanks to this app.

title: It didn’t figure out my American English
review: It needs refinement on the accent perception. I speak as a Native US English speaker. Haha

title: Great app! Effective and fun. A few feature enhancements needed.
review:
Great app! I have really enjoyed using this. It’s giving me more awareness of my voice. Your voice is an instrument and a deep part of your personality, and sometimes we are unaware of how we’re using it. This app is helping me use it the right way.
It could use a few feature enhancements. First, more adjectives are needed to rate other user’s voices, because sometimes, none are relevant. 
Secondly, it would be nice if it stored all of your voice recordings in a log, that way you could go back (even back months, years)  in time and compare your progress.

title: Too Expensive
review:
Could you please lower the price on the AI Voice Coach please, it’s too expensive and I want to be able to afford it,
Thanks,
JackNES85

title: I’m sorry but this isn’t simple to use
review: Life is about making things easier, that’s the whole point of technology but I find this app not user friendly. There are too many unnecessary features that makes it difficult to use or understand. Unless you have hours to play around just to figure out how to use it, I’d say get something else

title: Cool and weird
review: It’s pretty extensive with all the exercises, but the lessons are done by an AI generated person, which is a little weird at the same time. Overall it’s a pretty good app.

title: Fake Fake Fake!!
review: I have been trying to improve my voice using this app and I use the Voice Rating section to get what I thought was feedback from real people! I just found out the ratings from other users are mostly FAKE if not all fake! Instead of saying what you’re supposed to for the recording I recorded silence, no noise or speaking whatsoever. I received votes from other so called users stating my voice is Deep, Weak, Confident, thin etc! So they’re rating silence as a deep or Confident voice?? Think about how awful you’d have to be to take people trying to improve their voice and give fake ratings as to how their voice sounds. I could be thinking my voice is great or horrible based on this fake feedback. Imagine someone getting bad ratings and never doing that podcast they planned to do or pursue a dream they intended all because they got poor fake feedback. Extremely disappointed by this! Download the free trial and see for yourself!

The video for the beginning exercise that shows of a woman making air puffs and sucking air actually deflating the lungs rapidly and sucking the stomach area having no support mechanism.

title: I can’t get over how good this app is!
review:
I’m a non-binary professional voice teacher, n I’m absolutely amazed at how good this app is.  While I don’t particularly recommend it for beginners (unless under the guidance of an experienced coach/teacher), I DO recommend it for intermediate n advanced singers/public speakers.

I ESPECIALLY recommend this app for transmasc n masc afab non-binary people, bc it doesn’t put a person into the ‘girl’ box.  It just analyzes the voice.   For trans femme ppl, I don’t recommend working with this app bc it focuses a lot on deepening the voice, which can create a lot of gender dysphoria unless u r working alongside a teacher who has been trained in voice modification.

I ALSO recommend this app for cis women n men who identify as contraltos n basses!  I’m a (non-binary) contralto on the side of tenor who has always been told to talk n sing higher, lighter, brighter.  This app WILL NOT force u to do that!  It seems to have no considerations of gender whatsoever, n truly allows the voice to sit where it wants to sit!

Tl;dr HIGHLY recommended for professionals n experienced intermediate singers/actors, recommended for beginners under the guidance of a teacher.  Recommended for transmasc n afab non-binary ppl, not so much for trans femme ppl unless under the guidance of a teacher.  HIGHLY recommended for contraltos n basses!!!!
